---
title: "Airbnb quantitative analysis "
author: "Christophe Benavent"
date: today
institute: "Université Dauphine - PSL / DRM / ACSS-PSAI" 
format: 
  beamer: 
    aspectratio: 43
    navigation: horizontal
    theme: CambridgeUS
    colortheme: default
    fig-pos: 'center'
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 5
    incremental: true 
header-includes:
  - |
    \definecolor{DeepBlue}{RGB}{10,35,92}
    \definecolor{Bordeaux}{RGB}{125,24,54}
    \setbeamercolor{structure}{fg=DeepBlue}
    \setbeamercolor{palette primary}{bg=DeepBlue,fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{palette secondary}{bg=Bordeaux,fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{palette tertiary}{bg=DeepBlue,fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{frametitle}{fg=DeepBlue}
    \setbeamercolor{title}{fg=Bordeaux}
    \setbeamercolor{alerted text}{fg=Bordeaux}
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  error: false
editor: visual
bibliography: references.bib
---

```{r}

library(tidyverse)

# Warm Palette B (project-wide graph style)
pal_b <- c("#A3A381", "#D7CE93", "#EFEBCE", "#D8A48F", "#BB8487")
pal_b_seq <- c("#FFF8EE", "#F9E8D5", "#F1D2BD", "#E3B793", "#C89062", "#A86845")

theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#EFEBCE", color = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#EFEBCE", color = NA),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#D7CE93", linewidth = 0.35),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(color = "#362B26", face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(color = "#362B26"),
      axis.text = element_text(color = "#4A3A33")
    )
)

library(readr)
library(kableExtra)
library(cld3)
# Load the treemap library for visualization
library(treemapify)
library(treemap)
library(cowplot)


# Chargement des données
listings <- read_csv("./data/listings.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    price_numeric = as.numeric(gsub("\\$", "", price)),
    accommodates_recod = ifelse(accommodates > 6, 7, accommodates)
  )

# Chargement des données
# Load the reviews dataset and remove HTML line breaks from comments
reviews <- read_csv("./data/reviews_select.csv") %>%
  mutate(comments = str_replace_all(comments, "<br/>", ". "))  # Clean comments by removing  tags




reviews_short<-  reviews |>
  select(listing_id,date, reviewer_id)







```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Data structure

Hosts(t) \<- listings (t) -\> reviews -\> Guest

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Listings

```{r}

# Médianes pour accommodates_recod
medians_accommodates <- listings %>%
  group_by(accommodates_recod) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(price_numeric, na.rm = TRUE))

# Médianes pour room_type
medians_room_type <- listings %>%
  group_by(room_type) %>%
  summarise(median_price = median(price_numeric, na.rm = TRUE))

# Graphique 1 : par accommodates_recod
p1 <- ggplot(listings, aes(x = price_numeric)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "#A3A381", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(
    data = medians_accommodates,
    aes(xintercept = median_price),
    color = "#BB8487",
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max(listings$price_numeric, na.rm = TRUE), by = 50)) +
  labs(x = "Prix", y = "Fréquence", title = "Histogramme des prix par nombre de voyageurs") +
  theme_minimal() +
  facet_grid(accommodates_recod ~ ., scales = "free_y")

# Graphique 2 : par room_type
p2 <- ggplot(listings, aes(x = price_numeric)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "#A3A381", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(
    data = medians_room_type,
    aes(xintercept = median_price),
    color = "#BB8487",
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max(listings$price_numeric, na.rm = TRUE), by = 50)) +
  labs(x = "Prix", y = "Fréquence", title = "Histogramme des prix par type de logement") +
  theme_minimal() +
  facet_grid(room_type ~ ., scales = "free_y")

combined_plot <- plot_grid(p1, p2, ncol = 1)




ggsave(
  filename = "Images/combined_histograms.jpeg",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 27,
  height = 17,
  units = "cm",
  dpi = 300
)

```

![](Images/combined_histograms.jpeg)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}

#on calcule le nombre d'avis par logement
Avis<-reviews_short %>%
  filter(listing_id != 1) %>% 
  group_by(listing_id) %>%
  summarise(avis = n())

mean<-round(mean(Avis$avis),1)
median<-median(Avis$avis)
max<- max(Avis$avis)
g1<-ggplot(Avis, aes(x=avis+1)) +geom_histogram(binwidth=.1,fill="#BB8487")+
  theme_minimal()+ scale_x_log10()+
  annotate("text", x=200, y=2000, size=3,label= paste0("moyenne=",mean,"- médiane=", median,"- max=",max))+
  labs(x = "nombre d'avis par  offre", y = "Fréquences", title = "Distribution du nombre d'avis par offre", caption = "")


library(ineq)
library(gglorenz)

#on analyse la concentration
gini<-round(ineq(Avis$avis,type = c("Gini")),2)

g2<-Avis %>%
    ggplot(aes(avis)) +
    stat_lorenz(desc = TRUE,size=1.2,color="#BB8487") +
    coord_fixed() +
    geom_abline(linetype = "dashed") +
    theme_minimal() +labs(x = "Part cumulée des offres",
         y = "Part cumulée des avis",
         title = "Concentration du nombre d'avis par offre",
         caption = "") +
  annotate("text", x=.35, y=.6, size=3,label= paste0("indice de Gini=",gini))

cowplot::plot_grid(g1, g2, ncol = 2)


```

---


# Hosts

Un hôte peut détenir plusieurs logements. Les amateurs et les pros. Quelle concentration ?

```{r}

listings <- read_csv("./data/listings.csv")

hosts<- listings |>
  group_by(host_id) |>
  summarise(n=n()) |>
  mutate(nb_logm=ifelse(n==1,"1",
                       ifelse(n==2 | n==3 ,"2-3",
                              ifelse(n>3 & n<11,"4-10",
                                            ifelse(n>10 & n<50, "11-50", ">50")))),
         nb_logm = factor(nb_logm, levels = c("1", "2-3", "4-10", "11-50", ">50"), ordered = TRUE))


```

\tiny

```{r}

foo <- hosts %>%
  group_by(nb_logm) %>%
  summarise(
    m = n(),
    sum = sum(n),
    .groups = "drop"  # Évite un message d'avertissement sur les groupes
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    m_pct = 100*round(m / sum(m), 3),
    m_sum = 100*round(sum / sum(sum), 3)
  )

foo %>%
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    caption = "Distribution des offres par nombre d'offres gérées",
    col.names = c(
      "Nombre de Logements gérés par hôte",
      "Nombre d'hôtes",
      "Nombre de logements",
      "Hôtes %",
      "Logement % "
    ),
    align = "r",  # Alignement centré pour toutes les colonnes
    digits = 1 ,   # Pour afficher 3 décimales si nécessaire
    booktabs = TRUE  # Active le style booktabs (lignes horizontales minimalistes)
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position"),
    full_width = TRUE,
    position = "center"  # Place la légende en bas

  )

 # Sauvegarder le résultat dans un fichier .tex
 write.csv(
  foo,
  file = "tableau_distribution.csv",
  row.names = FALSE,  # Évite d'écrire les numéros de ligne
  fileEncoding = "UTF-8"  # Encodage pour éviter les problèmes de caractères spéciaux
)
 
```

\normalsize

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Synthesis

-   96 000 logements proposés à Paris en juin 2024, veille des jeux Olympiques.

-   72 000 hosts

-   530 000 reviews depuis 1 an.

-   230 000 guests

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reviews

-   Reviewers are described only by their surname, we used a small lmm to classify tham as Woman, Man, Couple, or company with an error correction component to improve the qualification.
-   The reviews are in many languages. The language detection is performed using cld3 (@ojo_divers-bench_2025). We categorize the languages into 7 major groups.(we have to improve the ontology) Here is the representation in the form of a treemap.
-   The reviews could be long or short

# Guest

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Gender

\tiny

```{r}

sentiment_analysis_results <- read_csv("data/sentiment_analysis_results.csv") |>
  select(langue, langue_short, sentiment, sentiment_score)

first1000_gender <- read_csv("data/first1000_gender.csv")

foo<-cbind(first1000_gender,sentiment_analysis_results) |>
  filter(gender!="Entreprise")
table(first1000_gender$gender, first1000_gender$confidence_score)

```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Sentiment

```{r}

fit<-lm(sentiment_score~gender*langue_short, foo)
summary(fit)
library(jtools)
library(interactions)
```

\normalsize

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}

effect_plot(fit,pred="gender")

```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}

effect_plot(fit,pred="langue_short")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}

cat_plot(fit, pred="langue_short", modx="gender")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Langages

```{r}


# Filter for reviews after June 13, 2023, and add language and word count features
reviews <- reviews %>%
  filter(date > as.Date("2023-06-13")) %>%  # Keep only recent reviews
  mutate(
    langue = detect_language(comments),  # Detect language of each comment
    # Categorize languages into broader groups
    langue_short = ifelse(langue == "en", "English",
                         ifelse(langue == "fr", "French",
                               ifelse(langue %in% c("it", "es", "pt", "ca", "ro"), "Latin",
                                     ifelse(langue %in% c("de", "nl"), "Germanic", 
                                            ifelse(langue %in% c("zh", "ja","ko"), "East Asia", "Others"))))),
    nwords = str_count(comments, "\\w+")  # Count words in each comment
  )

# Save the processed data to a new CSV file
write.csv(reviews, "./data/reviews_select.csv")

```

```{r}
# Calcul de la moyenne et de la médiane
mean_nwords <- mean(reviews$nwords, na.rm = TRUE)
median_nwords <- median(reviews$nwords, na.rm = TRUE)

# Création du graphique avec les lignes verticales
ggplot(reviews, aes(nwords)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#D8A48F", binwidth = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution des avis en fonction de leur longueur en nombre de mots") +
  # Ligne verticale pour la moyenne
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = mean_nwords),
    color = "#BB8487",  # Couleur rose-rouge pour la moyenne
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  # Label pour la moyenne
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = mean_nwords,
    y = Inf,
    label = paste0("Moyenne = ", round(mean_nwords, 1)),
    vjust = 2,
    hjust = 0.5,
    color = "#BB8487"
  ) +
  # Ligne verticale pour la médiane
  geom_vline(
    aes(xintercept = median_nwords),
    color = "#BB8487",  # Couleur rouge pour la médiane
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  # Label pour la médiane
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = median_nwords,
    y = Inf,
    label = paste0("Médiane = ", round(median_nwords, 1)),
    vjust = 1.0,
    hjust = 0.5,
    color = "#BB8487"
  )+
  labs(x="nombre de mots")+
  xlim(0,200)

# Sauvegarder le plot en JPEG avec une taille de 27x17 cm
ggsave(
  filename = "./Images/distribution_size_reviews.jpg",  # Nom du fichier de sortie
  plot = last_plot(),                                # Le plot à sauvegarder
  device = "jpeg",                         # Format JPEG
  width = 27,                              # Largeur en cm
  height = 17,                             # Hauteur en cm
  units = "cm",                            # Unité de mesure
  dpi = 300                                # Résolution (DPI)
)


```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}

# Aggregate data by language group and language: count reviews and average word count
foo <- reviews %>%
  group_by(langue_short, langue) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), nwords = mean(nwords)) |>
  mutate(n=as.numeric(n)) |>
  filter(!is.na(langue))


ggplot(foo, aes(area = n, fill = nwords, label = langue,
                subgroup = langue_short)) +
  geom_treemap() +
  geom_treemap_subgroup_border() +
  geom_treemap_subgroup_text(place = "centre", grow = T, alpha = 0.5, colour =
                             "grey", fontface = "italic", min.size = 0) +
  geom_treemap_text(colour = "white", place = "topleft", reflow = T)+
  labs(title="The distribution of reviews by language and word count")+
  # Inverser le gradient de couleur (rouge-bleu-vert au lieu de vert-bleu-rouge)
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = pal_b_seq
  )


  
# Sauvegarder le plot en JPEG avec une taille de 27x17 cm
ggsave(
  filename = "./Images/distribution_language_reviews.jpg",  # Nom du fichier de sortie
  plot = last_plot(),                                # Le plot à sauvegarder
  device = "jpeg",                         # Format JPEG
  width = 27,                              # Largeur en cm
  height = 17,                             # Hauteur en cm
  units = "cm",                            # Unité de mesure
  dpi = 300                                # Résolution (DPI)
)
        
```

```{r}
```

## Cross

# References

\tiny
